purpose: Full project context for external agent review. Public open-core repository — parser, eval, handoff only; editor removed per competitive IP review.

repository.name: steprs-dev
repository.license_proprietary: Geometry editor, agent layer, brepkit kernel, MCP — private repository (not in public git)

external_review.verdict: Technically sound; competitive posture fixed by repo split

open_core_boundary.note: Editor/agent/brepkit source removed from public repo; API routes are 503 stubs

external_review.recommendation_executed: Open-source parser + AAG handoff; lock editor and agent orchestration in private repository

product.name: steprs.dev
product.tagline: Client-side STEP CAM triage — check header, holes, stock, export AAG
not_claimed: Production geometry editing on public site
product.primary_user: Machinists and CAM programmers preparing STEP files before programming
product.privacy_model: Parse runs in browser Web Worker + Rust WASM; no STEP upload path in production workflow

architecture.summary: Single Rust forward pass produces ParseResult; Next.js workbench displays results; OCCT is viewport preview only.

pipeline_stages.name: ingest
pipeline_stages.work: DATA scan, nom entity parse, adaptive arena, fail-fast missing DATA, skip malformed entities

pipeline_stages.name: quoting
pipeline_stages.work: Units, part_envelope_mm (1st–99th percentile trim), holes, fillets, pockets, slots, setups

architecture.wasm_build: wasm-pack --no-default-features (parse-only; no editor APIs in bundle)

removed_from_public_git: apps/web/lib/editor/agent, ollama, intent-planner, part-intelligence, context, clarify, training, server-session
proprietary_boundary.private_repo_contains: NL agent, EditOp planner, brepkit edit kernel, MCP tools, editor WASM (editor feature build)
proprietary_boundary.history_scrubbed: git filter-repo executed; proprietary paths removed from all public commits and force-pushed. Full-history private copy retained locally (steprs-private).

features.header_check: STEP HEADER: AP203/AP214/AP242 hints, units, assembly byte-scan markers
features.holes: Heuristic cylindrical features from B-rep; drill/endmill catalog; kinds: through, blind, countersink
features.stock: part_envelope_mm, billet snap, allowance — not OCCT raw bbox
features.aag_export: Face adjacency with concave/convex/smooth; compact graph for handoff
features.preflight: CAM Pre-Flight tab: machine envelope fit, vise jaw/Z-stack clearance, tool crib reach (shank/holder collision), flat-bottom blind hole flags, undercut/5-axis warnings, machinability score, SFM-based starting feeds (apps/web/lib/preflight/)
features.editor: Not in public repo — Coming soon UI + 503 API stubs only

gates: Gate 2: hole counts, kinds, units, part_envelope bbox, AAG bounds
evaluation.preflight_rule_matrix: apps/web/lib/preflight/preflight-rules.eval.json — pins machine/tool/rule outcomes on real fixtures; runs in web CI (test:unit)
evaluation.synthetic_suite: scripts/synthetic/generate_synthetic_steps.py — cadquery-generated STEP files with known ground truth (truth.json) for differential parser testing

known_limitations: Hole metrics are B-rep geometry unless AP242 semantic PMI entities are present.
FTC-08/09/11 may report zero holes when bore rims fail confirmation.
Partial-arc holes (<180°) and sketch-extrude cuts may be missed or misclassified.
HTC geometry is FreeCAD-authored (fixture id: htc_freecad_geometry).
FreeCAD through-cuts may classify as countersink when conical face caps are present.

strengths: Rust ParseResult is machining truth; OCCT is display-only
12 unique strict golden fixtures with Gate 1 hole identity
part_envelope_mm aligned with stock golden gates
Parse-only WASM; editor IP removed from public git
Honest scope; rule-based labels; no ML oversell
Parser tests: DATA fail-fast, escapes, malformed skip, assembly header scan
risks: Root package.json license field says Apache-2.0 but workbench shell is mixed — see NOTICE
Machine/tool/workholding profiles are catalog approximations — users must verify
Handoff prompt and compactAagGraph are public by design

agent_review_questions: Is the open-core vs proprietary boundary enforced at repository level (not just runtime)?
Is the golden eval narrative honest (n=12 unique strict fixtures)?
Does public copy oversell DFM, PMI, ML, or verified editing?
Is part_envelope_mm the stock truth (not raw bbox or OCCT)?
Are parser edge cases tested (DATA fail-fast, STEP string escapes, malformed skip)?
Is the handoff API a credible public contract without leaking edit moat?
Was git history scrubbed before competitive editor launch?
